statement: For baseline prompt primitives, renderer-only prompt metadata keys are label, prompt, help, hint, and examples. Runtime-meaningful prompt metadata keys are default_value, prefill, default_expr, prefill_expr, and autofill_if.
heading_path: AudioMason2 - Wire Contracts Specification (Authoritative) > 10.23 Primitive Semantics (Baseline v1)

statement: For baseline prompt primitives, prompt metadata evaluation order is fixed and deterministic: literal default_value and prefill are interpreted first; default_expr is evaluated next; prefill_expr is evaluated after that; autofill_if is evaluated last. default_expr, prefill_expr, and autofill_if, when present, MUST be ExprRef values evaluated in PHASE 1 using only roots allowed by the expression model. autofill_if MUST be evaluated after prompt metadata normalization, before interactive rendering, and MUST resolve to bool.
heading_path: AudioMason2 - Wire Contracts Specification (Authoritative) > 10.23 Primitive Semantics (Baseline v1)

statement: ui.message@1 MUST NOT expose, consume, or imply prompt metadata semantics. It is non-interactive, accepts no submit payload, produces empty outputs {}, and the prompt metadata keys label, prompt, help, hint, examples, default_value, prefill, default_expr, prefill_expr, and autofill_if have no runtime meaning for ui.message@1.
heading_path: AudioMason2 - Wire Contracts Specification (Authoritative) > 10.23 Primitive Semantics (Baseline v1)

statement: Import plugin MUST declare a canonical transition operator set and semantics for import wizard transitions. Each operator MUST define operator_id, arity, operand type constraints, and deterministic semantics. The canonical import operator set MUST include at least: unconditional (always matches, arity 0), always_true (arity 0), always_false (arity 0), equals (arity 2, strict equality), not_equals (arity 2, strict inequality), exists (arity 1, value is present), not_exists (arity 1, value is absent).
heading_path: AudioMason2 - Project Specification (Authoritative) > 10.17 Interpreter Authority (Single Execution Engine)
